proofs.proof_tex:
Proof. Write the pooled centered cross-fit process as
\[
G_{cf}(\pi)=\sum_{k=1}^K w_k H_k(\pi),\qquad w_k=|I_k|/n,
\]
where \(H_k(\pi)\) is the centered evaluation-fold empirical average with the foldwise nuisance estimates held fixed. Conditional on the training folds, the increments in each \(H_k\) have envelope \(B\) and conditional second moment bounded by \(C B^2P_X(D_\pi)\), by the hypothesis of the lemma. The atomic offset assumption ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope is stated precisely for these finite-VC localized classes, including fixed-\(K\) cross-fitted evaluation-fold averages conditional on the training folds. Applying it foldwise gives
\[
E\left[\sup_{\pi\in\Pi}\{2|H_k(\pi)|-R_P(\pi)/4\}_+\mid \mathcal T_k\right]
\le C(B^2/n_k)^{A_\alpha}(\log n_k)^p,
\]
where \(n_k=|I_k|\). The regret localization required by that assumption is supplied by thm:margin-localization, whose bounded-outcome prerequisite is part of the displayed dependency list.

For the pooled process, use \(|\sum_k w_kH_k(\pi)|\le\sum_kw_k|H_k(\pi)|\) and convexity of \(x\mapsto x_+\):
\[
\{2|G_{cf}(\pi)|-R_P(\pi)/4\}_+
\le \sum_{k=1}^K w_k\{2|H_k(\pi)|-R_P(\pi)/4\}_+.
\]
Taking the supremum over \(\pi\), expectation, and then summing the foldwise bounds yields
\[
E_P\sup_{\pi\in\Pi}\{2|G_{cf}(\pi)|-R_P(\pi)/4\}_+
\le C\sum_{k=1}^K w_k(B^2/n_k)^{A_\alpha}(\log n_k)^p.
\]
By ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count, \(K\) is fixed and the deterministic folds are balanced, so \(n_k\asymp n\) and \(\log n_k\le C\log n\). Hence the last display is at most
\[
C(B^2/n)^{A_\alpha}(\log n)^p.
\]
This proves the claimed offset control. No propensity-stability, oracle-denominator, or marginal-overlap condition is used.

Proof. Set
\[
S=\sup_{\pi\in\Pi}\{2|z_\pi|-R_P(\pi)/4\}_+ .
\]
For the selected policy \(\widetilde\pi\), the assumed inequality gives
\[
R_P(\widetilde\pi)\le 2|z_{\widetilde\pi}|+\delta_n .
\]
Subtracting \(R_P(\widetilde\pi)/4\) from both sides gives
\[
\frac34R_P(\widetilde\pi)
\le 2|z_{\widetilde\pi}|-\frac14R_P(\widetilde\pi)+\delta_n
\le S+\delta_n .
\]
Therefore
\[
R_P(\widetilde\pi)\le \frac43S+\frac43\delta_n .
\]
Taking expectations and using the proved offset-control node lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control gives
\[
E_PR_P(\widetilde\pi)
\le C(B^2/n)^{A_\alpha}(\log n)^p+C\delta_n
=C\{\rho_n+\delta_n\}.
\]
Finally, \(A_\alpha=(1+\alpha)/(2+\alpha)<1\) for finite \(\alpha\ge0\) and \(B\ge1\), so for all large \(n\), \(n^{-1}\le C(B^2/n)^{A_\alpha}(\log n)^p\) after increasing the constant. Thus any \(\delta_n\le1/n\) is absorbed into the \(C\rho_n\) term.

Proof. Fix \(P\) in def:law-class and fix the cross-fitted nuisance estimates satisfying the estimator-side conditions in the statement. Let \(\widehat\pi=\widehat\pi_n\). The proof is conditional on the training folds; all bounds are uniform in the folds and are then integrated over the training randomness.

For \(i\in I_k\), write
\[
\Gamma_i=\Gamma_q(O_i;\widehat\eta^{(-k)}),\qquad
m_k(x)=E_P[\Gamma_q(O;\widehat\eta^{(-k)})\mid X=x]=\tau_P(x)+b_k(x).
\]
The identity defining \(b_k\) is exactly lem:clip-bias applied with the foldwise nuisance triple. By lem:feasible-erm-basic-inequality, with comparator \(\pi_\star\in\Pi\) from ass:optimal-in-class,
\[
P_n[(\widehat\pi-\pi_\star)\Gamma_i]\ge -1/n .
\]
Define the pooled centered process
\[
G_{cf}(\pi)=P_n[(\pi-\pi_\star)\Gamma_i]-\sum_{k=1}^Kw_kP[(\pi-\pi_\star)m_k],\qquad w_k=|I_k|/n.
\]
Using def:welfare-regret and thm:welfare-identity,
\[
P[(\pi-\pi_\star)\tau_P]=-R_P(\pi),
\]
so the basic inequality gives, with \(r=R_P(\widehat\pi)\),
\[
r\le |G_{cf}(\widehat\pi)|+\left|\sum_{k=1}^Kw_kP[(\widehat\pi-\pi_\star)b_k]\right|+1/n .
\]
The stochastic process is controlled only through the crude clipped-score envelope. Lem:crude-clipped-score-envelope gives \(|\Gamma_i|\le C/q\), and since \(\pi-\pi_\star\) vanishes outside \(D_\pi\), the centered increments have envelope \(B=C/q\) and conditional second moment bounded by \(CB^2P_X(D_\pi)\). Hence lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control applies to \(G_{cf}\), and lem:localized-vc-self-bound will control the random-radius choice \(\widehat\pi\) once the deterministic drift is absorbed.

Assume \(\gamma>0\) and \(q\le c_ou^\gamma\). Lem:localized-clipped-drift-bound, applied foldwise and averaged over \(k\), gives
\[
\left|\sum_{k=1}^Kw_kP[(\widehat\pi-\pi_\star)b_k]\right|
\le C\left\{\frac{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}}q+r_{\mu,n}u^{\alpha/2}q^{1/(2\gamma)}+r_{\mu,n}(r/u)^{1/2}\right\}.
\]
By Young's inequality,
\[
Cr_{\mu,n}(r/u)^{1/2}\le r/4+Cr_{\mu,n}^2/u .
\]
Absorbing \(r/4\) into the left side yields
\[
r\le 2|G_{cf}(\widehat\pi)|+C\left\{\frac{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}}q+r_{\mu,n}u^{\alpha/2}q^{1/(2\gamma)}+\frac{r_{\mu,n}^2}{u}+\frac1n\right\}.
\]
Now apply lem:localized-vc-self-bound with \(B=C/q\). Since \(q\le1/2\) and \(A_\alpha\le1\), the \(1/n\) term is absorbed by the stochastic term for all large \(n\). This gives
\[
E_PR_P(\widehat\pi_n)
\le C\left\{(nq^2)^{-A_\alpha}+\frac{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}}q+r_{\mu,n}u^{\alpha/2}q^{1/(2\gamma)}+\frac{r_{\mu,n}^2}{u}\right\}(\log n)^p .
\]
The bounded oracle policy-learning centered process with score \((\pi-\pi_\star)\tau_P\) has envelope bounded by a universal constant and second moment bounded by \(CP_X(D_\pi)\); the same localized VC argument gives an additional valid oracle/localization contribution \(n^{-A_\alpha}(\log n)^p\). Because \(\beta_{\alpha,γ}\ge0\), def:exponents gives \(r_\star=(1+\alpha)/(2+\alpha+\beta_{\alpha,γ})\le A_\alpha\), so \(n^{-A_\alpha}\le n^{-r_\star}\). Retaining this benchmark term explicitly gives
\[
E_PR_P(\widehat\pi_n)
\le C\left\{n^{-r_\star}+(nq^2)^{-A_\alpha}+\frac{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}}q+r_{\mu,n}u^{\alpha/2}q^{1/(2\gamma)}+\frac{r_{\mu,n}^2}{u}\right\}(\log n)^p .
\]

For \(\gamma=0\), ass:strict-overlap-endpoint and fixed \(q\le\underline p/2\) imply, by lem:localized-clipped-drift-bound,
\[
\left|\sum_{k=1}^Kw_kP[(\widehat\pi-\pi_\star)b_k]\right|\le Cr_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}.
\]
The clipped-score envelope is then constant, so the same offset/self-bound argument gives
\[
E_PR_P(\widehat\pi_n)\le C\{n^{-A_\alpha}+r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}\}(\log n)^p .
\]
The proof uses the single data-driven ERM in def:feasible-erm and the crude \(q^{-2}\) second-moment scale; it does not use a marginal-tail shortcut, known propensity, oracle denominator, new law class, or clipped-propensity variance-stability premise.

Proof. The causal target is the welfare regret \(R_P(\pi)=V_P(\pi_\star)-V_P(\pi)\), whose identifying functional is fixed in def:welfare-regret. This result concerns the estimation behavior of the explicit cross-fit clipped-AIPW \(1/n\)-ERM in def:feasible-erm.

Fix one nuisance regime \((a,c,C_\mu,C_{prod})\). The upper-risk functional in def:upper-risk takes the supremum over laws in def:law-class together with the explicitly stated estimator-side and policy-side conditions: ass:optimal-in-class, ass:nuisance-rate, ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances, ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents, ass:policy-class, ass:vc-localized-envelope, ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope, and ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count. These conditions are not asserted to follow from def:law-class.

First let \(\gamma>0\). Def:feasible-rate chooses \(q_n=q_0n^{-s_{feas}}\) and \(u_n=\bar u n^{-t_{feas}}\) on the compact feasible set \(0\le s_{feas}\le1/2\), \(0\le t_{feas}\le s_{feas}/\gamma\). Hence \(q_n\le c_ou_n^\gamma\) for all large \(n\), and lem:crude-localized-master-bound applies:
\[
E_PR_P(\widehat\pi_n)
\le C\left\{n^{-r_\star}+(nq_n^2)^{-A_\alpha}+\frac{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}}{q_n}+r_{\mu,n}u_n^{\alpha/2}q_n^{1/(2\gamma)}+\frac{r_{\mu,n}^2}{u_n}\right\}(\log n)^p.
\]
By lem:clip-balance-exponent and ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents, the four feasible stochastic/nuisance terms are bounded by \(Cn^{-g_{joint}(\alpha,\gamma,a,c)}\). Therefore
\[
E_PR_P(\widehat\pi_n)
\le Cn^{-\min\{r_\star,g_{joint}\}}(\log n)^p
=Cn^{-r_{up}}(\log n)^p,
\]
where \(r_{up}=r_{feas}\) is the exponent constructed in def:feasible-rate.

For \(\gamma=0\), def:feasible-rate uses fixed \(q_n=q_0\le\underline p/2\). Lem:crude-localized-master-bound gives
\[
E_PR_P(\widehat\pi_n)\le C\{n^{-A_\alpha}+r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}\}(\log n)^p.
\]
Since ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents gives \(r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}\le Cn^{-c}\),
\[
E_PR_P(\widehat\pi_n)\le Cn^{-\min\{A_\alpha,c\}}(\log n)^p=Cn^{-r_{up}}(\log n)^p.
\]
Taking the supremum over the conditional domain in def:upper-risk proves
\[
U_n(\alpha,\gamma,a,c;\widehat\eta)\le Cn^{-r_{up}}(\log n)^p.
\]
This derives the closed honest exponent for the plain feasible clipped-AIPW ERM from the crude \(q^{-2}\) score envelope and the deterministic drift controls; no new law-class split or rate-buying propensity stability assumption is introduced.

added_lemmas.statement: For the deterministic schedule in def:feasible-rate, the terms in lem:crude-localized-master-bound optimize to the exponent r_up=r_feas. For gamma>0, with A_alpha=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha), q_n=q_0 n^{-s_feas}, u_n=u_bar n^{-t_feas}, and g_joint=max_{0<=s<=1/2, 0<=t<=s/gamma} min{A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)+alpha t/2, 2a-t}, the master bound is at most C{n^{-r_star}+n^{-g_joint}}(log n)^p = C n^{-min{r_star,g_joint}}(log n)^p. For gamma=0, the exponent is min{A_alpha,c}.
added_lemmas.proof_tex:
Proof. Let \(A_\alpha=(1+\alpha)/(2+\alpha)\). Suppose first that \(\gamma>0\). For a feasible schedule \(q_n=q_0n^{-s}\), \(u_n=\bar u n^{-t}\) with \(0\le s\le1/2\) and \(0\le t\le s/\gamma\), the normalization in def:feasible-rate gives \(q_n\le c_ou_n^\gamma\) for all large \(n\). The four non-oracle terms in lem:crude-localized-master-bound have exponents
\[
(nq_n^2)^{-A_\alpha}=C n^{-A_\alpha(1-2s)},
\]
\[
r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}/q_n\le Cn^{-(c-s)},
\]
\[
r_{\mu,n}u_n^{\alpha/2}q_n^{1/(2\gamma)}\le Cn^{-\{a+s/(2\gamma)+\alpha t/2\}},
\]
and
\[
r_{\mu,n}^2/u_n\le Cn^{-(2a-t)}.
\]
The last three displays use ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents. Thus, for this \((s,t)\), the feasible part of the master bound is at most \(Cn^{-\phi(s,t)}\), where
\[
\phi(s,t)=\min\{A_\alpha(1-2s),c-s,a+s/(2\gamma)+\alpha t/2,2a-t\}.
\]
The function \(\phi\) is continuous on the compact feasible set, so the maximizer chosen in def:feasible-rate exists and has value \(g_{joint}\). Consequently the gamma-positive upper exponent is
\[
r_{up}=r_{feas}=\min\{r_\star(\alpha,\gamma),g_{joint}(\alpha,\gamma,a,c)\}.
\]
If \(\gamma=0\), the clip is fixed, so the stochastic term has exponent \(A_\alpha\), and ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents gives the product-nuisance exponent \(c\). Therefore \(r_{up}=r_{feas}=\min\{A_\alpha,c\}\).

open_obligations.what_is_open: In the strict-gap regime g_joint(alpha,gamma,a,c)<r_star(alpha,gamma), determine whether the slower conditional feasible exponent r_up=g_joint is intrinsic to genuinely feasible offline policy learning with estimated cross-fitted nuisances, or whether another feasible estimator can attain the lower-bound exponent r_star.
open_obligations.obstruction: The available converse thm:minimax-lower is a law-class lower bound for arbitrary measurable Pi-valued estimators and gives only c n^{-r_star}. It does not encode first-stage nuisance-estimation difficulty near weak-overlap arms, so it cannot rule out rates between n^{-r_star} and the proved clipped-AIPW upper rate n^{-g_joint}. Conversely, oeq:feasible-upper proves one explicit estimator's conditional risk bound and does not establish optimality among all feasible estimators using estimated nuisances.
open_obligations.attempted: The crude-envelope ERM route was optimized over the clip q_n and localization split u_n, yielding g_joint from the balance A_alpha(1-2s), c-s, a+s/(2 gamma)+alpha t/2, and 2a-t. The baseline fuzzy two-point lower-bound construction was compared against this exponent, but that construction varies the data law rather than the first-stage nuisance-learning mechanism and therefore does not close the strict-gap branch.
open_obligations.partial_result: If g_joint(alpha,gamma,a,c)>=r_star(alpha,gamma), then oeq:feasible-upper and thm:minimax-lower match up to logarithmic factors for the explicit feasible ERM: c n^{-r_star} <= M_n and U_n <= C n^{-r_star}(log n)^p. If g_joint<r_star, the current results give the honest bracket c n^{-r_star} for the baseline minimax lower benchmark and U_n <= C n^{-g_joint}(log n)^p for the specified feasible clipped-AIPW ERM, with tightness unresolved.
